skill.name: graphql-architect
skill.description: Guides GraphQL schema design, federation strategy, performance optimization, caching, security, subscriptions, testing, and enterprise API governance.
skill.summary: Design scalable, secure, and high-performance GraphQL APIs for enterprise systems.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these were prose matches in SKILL.md. No executable code, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Resource Reference
low_findings.description: Line 26 uses backticks around a relative markdown resource path and asks the assistant to open supporting material. This is not shell execution, does not invoke an interpreter, and does not pass user input to a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only evidence is markdown inline code around a document path. There is no Ruby syntax, subprocess call, shell metacharacter use, or executable payload.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts occur in natural-language GraphQL architecture text, including descriptions of schema design, federation, caching, and examples. No cryptographic algorithm configuration or implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of each reported line shows documentation prose rather than crypto code. There are no mentions of DES, MD5, SHA1, cipher selection, key handling, or hashing routines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Keyword
low_findings.description: Line 103 describes API gateway integration and hybrid architecture approaches. It does not contain host discovery, environment probing, network scanning, or system enumeration instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a benign capability statement about API architecture. No reconnaissance command, tool name, endpoint probing, or data collection behavior appears nearby.

content.value_statement: GraphQL systems can become slow, inconsistent, and hard to govern as teams and services grow. This skill guides schema design, federation, caching, security, and testing decisions for production APIs.
actual_capabilities: Guides schema-first GraphQL design with interfaces, unions, custom scalars, and connection patterns.
Plans federated GraphQL systems using Apollo Federation v2, subgraphs, gateways, and schema governance.
Recommends performance patterns such as DataLoader, query complexity limits, persisted queries, and caching.
Outlines security controls for authorization, rate limiting, introspection, input validation, and production hardening.
Supports real-time GraphQL planning with subscriptions, live queries, filtering, authorization, and scalable infrastructure.
Covers testing, schema validation, breaking change detection, monitoring, and operational analytics.
limitations: It provides architecture guidance and examples, not a complete implementation by itself.
It needs project context, data relationships, traffic targets, and team boundaries for precise recommendations.
It cannot verify runtime performance without application code, telemetry, or benchmark results.
It may need adaptation for specific GraphQL frameworks, hosting platforms, and organizational policies.

use_cases.title: Plan a Federated API Platform
use_cases.description: Define subgraph ownership, gateway behavior, schema contracts, and governance practices for a multi-team GraphQL platform.

use_cases.title: Improve Resolver Performance
use_cases.description: Identify N+1 risks, batching opportunities, cache boundaries, persisted query options, and query cost controls.

use_cases.title: Harden a Production GraphQL API
use_cases.description: Review authorization, introspection exposure, input validation, rate limits, subscription security, and operational monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Review a New Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my GraphQL schema goals and data model. Suggest types, relationships, naming conventions, and validation rules for a maintainable first version.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Query Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this GraphQL API for performance risks. Focus on resolver batching, caching layers, query depth, complexity limits, and monitoring signals.

prompt_templates.title: Design Federation Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a federated GraphQL architecture for these services and teams. Recommend subgraph boundaries, ownership rules, gateway strategy, and schema governance.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Production Readiness Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production readiness plan for this GraphQL platform. Include security controls, testing strategy, migration risks, observability, caching, and rollout steps.

output_examples.input: Design a GraphQL API for a commerce platform with product, inventory, order, and customer services.
output: A recommended subgraph ownership model for each domain.
Schema boundary guidance for shared entities and references.
Gateway, registry, and governance practices for safe evolution.
Operational concerns for performance, authorization, and monitoring.

output_examples.input: Find performance risks in a GraphQL API that has slow nested queries.
output: A prioritized list of likely N+1 resolver paths.
Batching and cache recommendations for high-traffic fields.
Query complexity, depth limit, and persisted query controls.
Telemetry signals to confirm improvement after changes.

output_examples.input: Prepare a GraphQL API for production security review.
output: A checklist covering authorization, introspection, input validation, and rate limiting.
Testing and monitoring recommendations for abuse detection.

best_practices: Provide schema goals, user flows, data ownership, and traffic expectations before asking for architecture recommendations.
Ask for tradeoffs between developer experience, performance, security, and backward compatibility.
Validate recommendations with schema checks, resolver tests, load tests, and production telemetry.
anti_patterns: Do not use one large graph without clear domain ownership or schema governance.
Do not expose unrestricted introspection, deep queries, or subscriptions in production without controls.
Do not optimize caching or batching without measuring resolver behavior and query patterns.

faq.question: Does this skill write GraphQL server code?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation choices and examples, but its main purpose is architecture, design review, and production planning.

faq.question: Which GraphQL tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Apollo Server, Apollo Federation, GraphQL Yoga, Pothos, Nexus, Prisma, TypeGraphQL, Hasura, PostGraphile, Relay, and Apollo Client.

faq.question: Can it help with GraphQL federation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can plan subgraphs, gateway configuration, ownership boundaries, schema composition, and governance practices.

faq.question: Can it improve GraphQL performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on DataLoader, batching, caching, query complexity, persisted queries, resolver tracing, and monitoring.

faq.question: Can it help with GraphQL security?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers authorization, rate limiting, input validation, introspection hardening, CORS, headers, and subscription access control.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide schema drafts, service boundaries, data sources, traffic expectations, client needs, security requirements, and deployment constraints.
